steps.title: Identify the subject and its number
steps.content: The main subject is The Proto-Atlantic-Congo language (singular). The phrase “common ancestor of 1,453 African languages with similar linguistic properties” is an appositive that renames the subject but doesn't change its number. Key signal: singular subject.

steps.title: Apply the subject-verb agreement rule
steps.content: Subject-verb agreement requires a singular subject to take a singular verb. Is (B) is the singular form that satisfies this requirement. The other choices violate the rule by using plural forms or the wrong auxiliary. That matches choice B — B is correct.
